user_query:
Please read input/usecases.jsonl and input/more_usecases.csv, merge the records, and deduplicate by title (case-insensitive, trim whitespace). Only keep entries whose category is exactly one of: ["Messaging & Communication Automation", "Calendar & Scheduling", "Remote Coding & Development", "Price Monitoring & Shopping", "Smart Home & IoT"]. If the same title appears in both sources, keep the JSONL version’s fields. Then produce three outputs:

1) Write a JSON summary to output/usecases_summary.json with this exact structure and keys: {
  "total_unique": <int>,
  "duplicates_removed": <int>,
  "categories": [ {"name": <string>, "count": <int>}, ... ],
  "source_counts": {"jsonl": <int>, "csv": <int>}
}. The categories array must contain exactly the five categories above with correct counts based on the merged, deduplicated set, and the sum of counts must equal total_unique.

2) Write a Markdown catalog to output/catalog_by_category.md grouped by category in alphabetical order by category name. For each category, include a header in the form "## <Category> (<count>)" followed by bullet lines sorted case-insensitive by title, each in the exact format: "- <Title> — <Platform> — Source: <URL>". Do not add extra fields or change the delimiter (use the long en dash — between fields).

3) Write a plain text validation report to output/validation_report.txt containing at least these three lines on their own lines: "Total unique: <int>", "Duplicates removed: <int>", and "Categories: 5".

Notes:
- Do not invent entries that are not present in the input files.
- Normalize whitespace in titles and categories before comparing for deduplication and sorting.
- Sorting by title should be case-insensitive.
- Make sure the numbers in the three files are consistent with one another.
- All file paths must be written under output/ exactly as specified.
